summarization.a: The figure shows the C 1s XPS spectrum of pristine graphene, with contributions from C sp², plasmon peaks, and C-O bonds.
summarization.b: The figure shows the C 1s XPS spectrum of H₂ plasma treated graphene for 5 minutes, with contributions from C sp² and C sp³.
summarization.c: The figure shows the C 1s XPS spectrum of O₂ plasma treated graphene for 5 minutes, with contributions from C sp², C sp³, C-O, and C=O.

data_extraction.a:
| Component | Binding Energy (eV) |
|---|---|
| C sp^2 | Peak at 284.5 |
| Plasmon 1 | Peak at 290 |
| Plasmon 2 | Peak at 293 |
| C-O | Peak at 286.5 |
data_extraction.b:
| Component | Binding Energy (eV) |
|---|---|
| C sp^2 | Peak at 284.5 |
| C sp^3 | Peak at 285 |
data_extraction.c:
| Component | Binding Energy (eV) |
|---|---|
| C sp^2 | Peak at 284.5 |
| C sp^3 | Peak at 285 |
| C-O | Peak at 286.5 |
| C=O | Peak at 289 |

a.question: What samples were analysed with XPS?
a.answer: 1) Pristine graphene, 2) H2-plasma treated graphene (5 min) and 3) O2-plasma treated graphene

a.question: How come a small component peak belonging to the C-O bond is obsereved in the prinstine graphene sample?
a.answer: This may be related to residual polymer residue in the sample or surface groups present at defects/surface sites.

b.question: How much of the sp^2 C bonds is converted to sp^3 bonds?

c.question: What function groups are likely formed after exposure to O2 plasma?
c.answer: Epoxy groups (C-O-C), hydroxyl groups (C-OH), carbonyl groups (C=O)
